ArchitectureNotes.objective: Provide a safe, transparent AI-powered system advisor that collects real system data, sends it to a configurable LLM, and executes only user-confirmed deletions through mole's existing safe_remove pipeline.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: The advisor does not replace existing mole commands. It augments them with LLM-driven analysis for users who want guided cleanup recommendations.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: LLM hallucination — model may suggest non-existent paths or dangerous operations. All paths validated before deletion.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Collector accuracy — du on SIP-protected dirs returns 0 or hangs. Timeout guards and _fast_du_sk_bg mitigate this.

contract.purpose: Fundamental constants, colors, icons, platform detection, and utility functions shared by all modules

notes.note-1: Pre-existing module. Not part of AI advisor scope but heavily depended upon.

contract.purpose: Source-chain loader that brings in all core modules. Provides shared initialization and update flow.

notes.note-1: Contains set -euo pipefail which affects all sourced files. AI advisor must use set +e after sourcing.

contract.purpose: safe_remove, validate_path_for_deletion, path size measurement. The trusted deletion pipeline.

notes.note-1: Critical dependency for AI advisor execution. All deletions go through safe_remove.

contract.purpose: Terminal UI primitives: read_key, hide/show_cursor, spinner, cursor control
should_protect_path — determines if a path is protected from deletion based on bundle IDs, system components, and critical data patterns

notes.note-1: Called by validate_path_for_deletion. Protects system settings, Notes, Finder, Dock, Bluetooth, WiFi, iCloud.

contract.purpose: Manage AI advisor configuration: endpoint URL, model name, API key, temperature, max_tokens, timeout. Stored in ~/.config/mole/ai.conf

fn-ai_config_is_configured.PURPOSE: Check if endpoint and model are set

contract.purpose: OpenAI-compatible HTTP client for chat completions API. Supports both standard and SSE streaming modes via curl + python3.

Gather system state: disk usage (user+system dirs), memory, CPU, trash, cleanable items, build artifacts, installer files, network, battery. Auto-sources collector_ext.sh for Docker/Homebrew/Xcode data.

fn-collector_run_all.PURPOSE: Run all collection sections to stdout, includes extended collectors

fn-_fast_du_sk.PURPOSE: Get directory size in KB via du -sk

fn-_fast_du_sk_bg.PURPOSE: Get directory size with background timeout guard

fn-_collect_section.PURPOSE: Output section header and call collector function

notes.note-1: Must run in subprocess (bash -c) due to set -e inheritance from common.sh.
notes.note-2: Auto-sources collector_ext.sh at end of collector_run_all.

contract.purpose: Extended system data collectors: Docker images/containers/volumes, Homebrew cache/outdated/cellar breakdown, Xcode simulators/archives/derived data.

fn-_collect_docker_info.PURPOSE: Docker disk usage, images by size, containers, volumes by size

fn-_collect_homebrew_info.PURPOSE: Homebrew cache, cellar top packages, outdated packages

fn-_collect_xcode_info.PURPOSE: Xcode simulators, derived data, archives, device support

contract.purpose: Define system prompt with risk taxonomy (SAFE/CAUTION/RISKY) and require structured response format: Disk Usage Summary table, Not Recommended section, Low Risk section, Medium/High Risk section, followed by JSON plan block.

fn-ai_system_prompt.PURPOSE: Output the system prompt to stdout

contract.purpose: Render AI advisor markdown report, parse JSON plan, interactive selection menu with risk filter (F key) and scrolling, confirmation prompt.

fn-_render_report.PURPOSE: Parse LLM markdown and render formatted report sections

fn-_extract_json_plan.PURPOSE: Extract JSON block from LLM response

fn-_parse_plan_items.PURPOSE: Parse JSON plan into pipe-delimited lines

fn-_load_plan.PURPOSE: Load parsed plan into parallel arrays

fn-_show_plan_menu.PURPOSE: Render [x]/[ ] menu with scrolling and filter label

fn-_interactive_select.PURPOSE: Keyboard selection with F key risk filter, scrolling

fn-_show_confirmation.PURPOSE: Typed yes confirmation with paths listing

fn-_update_visible_indices.PURPOSE: Recalculate visible items for current filter

fn-_cycle_risk_filter.PURPOSE: Cycle filter: empty -> SAFE -> CAUTION -> RISKY -> empty

contract.purpose: Execute confirmed plan items via safe_remove with glob expansion and per-item progress reporting.

fn-_expand_glob_paths.PURPOSE: Expand glob patterns to filesystem paths

fn-_execute_plan.PURPOSE: Execute selected items via safe_remove with size tracking

M-AI-ADVISOR.NAME: AI Advisor CLI

contract.purpose: Main advisor command. Orchestrates: config check, data collection (subprocess), API call with retry, report rendering, interactive [x]/[ ] menu, confirmation, execution via safe_remove. Supports --auto-safe flag.

param.name: --dry-run
param.description: Collect data only, no API call

fn-main.PURPOSE: Entry point: parse args, orchestrate flow

fn-_call_ai_with_retry.PURPOSE: Call AI with up to 2 retries on failure

M-AI-ADVISOR.depends: M-AI-CONFIG, M-AI-CLIENT, M-AI-COLLECTOR, M-AI-COLLECTOR-EXT, M-AI-PROMPT, M-AI-RENDERER, M-AI-EXECUTOR, M-FILE-OPS, M-UI, M-LOG

notes.note-1: Default selection is all unchecked. User must actively select items with Space.
notes.note-2: Confirmation requires typing "yes" — no accidental Enter confirmations.
notes.note-3: Glob paths (ending in /*) expand to directory contents and delete items inside, not the directory itself.
notes.note-4: --auto-safe auto-selects only SAFE items and skips interactive menu.
notes.note-5: API calls retry up to 2 times on failure with 2-second delay.

DF-ADVISE.NAME: Full Advisor Flow
DF-ADVISE.step-2: advisor.sh checks config (M-AI-CONFIG), prompts setup if missing
DF-ADVISE.step-3: collector_run_all (M-AI-COLLECTOR) runs in subprocess, auto-sources M-AI-COLLECTOR-EXT for Docker/Homebrew/Xcode
DF-ADVISE.step-4: _call_ai_with_retry sends prompt+data via M-AI-CLIENT (up to 2 retries)
DF-ADVISE.step-5: Response captured. Markdown report extracted and rendered via M-AI-RENDERER _render_report
DF-ADVISE.step-6: JSON plan block extracted via M-AI-RENDERER _extract_json_plan, parsed into arrays
DF-ADVISE.step-7: M-AI-RENDERER _interactive_select shows menu with F key risk filter and scrolling
DF-ADVISE.step-8: M-AI-RENDERER _show_confirmation lists selected paths, requires typed "yes"
DF-ADVISE.step-9: M-AI-EXECUTOR _execute_plan iterates paths, expands globs, calls safe_remove (M-FILE-OPS)
DF-ADVISE.step-10: Summary displayed: items removed, space recovered, items skipped
DF-ADVISE.evidence: Step progress output, report rendering, menu interaction, execution log

DF-CONFIG.NAME: Config Setup Flow
DF-CONFIG.step-2: ai_config_setup prompts for endpoint, model, API key, optional parameters

DF-DRY-RUN.NAME: Dry Run Flow
DF-DRY-RUN.step-4: No API call, no menu, no deletions
DF-DRY-RUN.evidence: Terminal output shows all collector sections

Phase-1.name: AI Advisor Core
Phase-1.goal: Working AI advisor with data collection, LLM integration, interactive menu, and safe execution
Phase-1.step-5: Main CLI command with report, menu, execution

Phase-2.name: Modularization & Extended Collection
Phase-2.goal: Extract renderer/executor into separate modules, add Docker/Homebrew/Xcode collectors, add UX features (risk filter, scrolling, --auto-safe, API retry)

Phase-2A.name: Module Extraction
Phase-2A.step-1: Extract _render_report, plan parsing, menu, confirmation into lib/ai/renderer.sh
Phase-2A.step-3: Update bin/advisor.sh to source new modules, remove inline functions

Phase-2B.name: Extended Collection
Phase-2B.step-3: Xcode info collector (simulators, derived data, archives)

Phase-2C.name: UX Polish
Phase-2C.step-1: --auto-safe flag: auto-select SAFE items, skip menu
Phase-2C.step-2: API retry: up to 2 retries with 2-second delay
Phase-2C.step-3: Risk filter: F key cycles SAFE/CAUTION/RISKY/all
Phase-2C.step-4: Scrolling: page-size 15, scroll offset for large lists

Phase-3.name: Testing & Distribution
Phase-3.goal: Comprehensive test coverage and integration with mole's distribution
Phase-3.step-1: bats tests for collector: mock du output, verify section format, timeout behavior
Phase-3.step-2: bats tests for JSON extraction, plan parsing, risk filter with various LLM response formats
Phase-3.step-3: bats tests for glob expansion, safe_remove integration
Phase-3.step-4: bats tests for Docker/Homebrew/Xcode collectors with mocked commands
Phase-3.step-5: Integration test: full flow with mocked API response

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope.
